title: caucalis gracilis subsp. umbrosa
intro: Caucalis gracilis subsp. umbrosa, also known as a type of wild carrot, is a perennial plant in the family Apiaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Caucalis gracilis subsp. umbrosa include reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its dried roots and leaves can be used in herbal remedies.
